details.publicNotes:
Conversion notes (this module)

Role: the AP's first villain and every PC's revenge payoff — a frail catalyst, not a tank. The real threat is the crocodile; Gaedren weaponizes it.

Tactics: an old leg wound caps his Speed at 15 ft, so he holds the far side of the gator pool and snipes rather than kiting. Round 1 Spur the Beast to enrage Gobblegut, then Strikes from cover; Nimble Dodge when focus-fired; flees to the A12 skiffs at ≤8 HP.

If killed and left in A14, his son Rolth animates the body in the Dead Warrens at the chapter's end — track it.

description.value: The most significant manifestation of Gaedren’s long life of crime is his limp; an old wound to his left leg that reduces his Speed to 15 feet.
This small, bladed weapon is held in one hand and used to stab a creature in close combat. It can also be thrown.

Sometimes referred to as an alley bow by rogues or ruffians, this small crossbow fires small bolts that are sometimes used to deliver poison to the target. It's small enough to be shot one-handed, but it still requires two hands to load.
Shorter than traditional arrows but similar in construction, bolts are the ammunition used by crossbows.
Shorter than traditional arrows but similar in construction, bolts are the ammunition used by crossbows.
This leather armor is reinforced with metal studs and sometimes small metal plates, providing most of the flexibility of leather armor with more robust protection.
(unlocks all doors in the fishery)
The nature of this object is unclear.
The nature of this object is unclear.

Gaedren isn't @Compendium[pf2e.conditionitems.Flat-Footed]{Flat-Footed} to creatures of 2nd level or lower that are @Compendium[pf2e.conditionitems.Hidden]{Hidden}, undetected, flanking, or using surprise attack.

Trigger A creature targets you with an attack and you can see the attacker.

Requirements You are not encumbered

You deftly dodge out of the way, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack.

Gaedren threatens and attempts a hand crossbow Strike against Gobblegut. If successful, Gobblegut becomes frenzied and gains +1 to attack rolls, damage rolls, and takes a -1 penalty to AC, saves, and skill checks until the start of Gaedren's next turn.

Gaedren deals [[/r 1d6]] extra precision damage to @Compendium[pf2e.conditionitems.Flat-Footed]{Flat-Footed} creatures.

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.SneakAttack]
On the first round of combat, creatures that haven't acted yet are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Flat-Footed} to Gaedren.

Trigger Gaedren reduces a creature to 0 Hit Points

Effect Gaerdren attempts an Intimidation check with a +2 circumstance bonus to @Compendium[pf2e.actionspf2e.Demoralize]{Demoralize} a single creature he can see and that can see him.
Gaedren's grimy padded armor carries a +1 armor potency rune — his PF1e '+1 padded armor', kept as the minor armor reward the Treasure page promises. His AC already includes it.
